search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae cough telehealth clinical history red flags investigations referral 2024
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic infection, asthma, COPD, reflux, or cancer diagnosis
automatic antibiotic, inhaler, imaging, lung-function test, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Cough has multiple acute and chronic causes.
No diagnosis, test, medicine, or action is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: DHA telehealth patients with cough; young children, pregnancy, immunocompromise, established lung disease, haemoptysis, severe breathlessness, and acute instability require tailored pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA telehealth evidence; emergency, face-to-face, paediatric, prescribing, imaging, pulmonary, and facility pathways remain locally governed.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA history section supports cough duration, quality, timing, triggers, risk factors, associated symptoms, and interval change without assigning a respiratory diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed severe, respiratory, systemic, bleeding, neurological, or deterioration concerns and available examination without asserting a negative.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation section supports recording already reviewed blood, chest radiograph, lung-function, sputum, ECG, or advanced-imaging context without ordering tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral section supports clinician-entered safety net, referral status, questions, and follow-up without assigning urgency automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.

unresolved_source_gaps: Current cough phenotype, observations, examination, cause, diagnosis, test, medicine, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
